dublinCore.dc:title: 119 SRES 476 IS: Supporting the designation of October 2025 as “Substance Use & Misuse Prevention Month” to raise awareness of substance use and misuse in the United States.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Ricketts (for himself and Mr. Schiff) submitted the following resolution; which was referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions

form.official-title: Supporting the designation of October 2025 as Substance Use & Misuse Prevention Month to raise awareness of substance use and misuse in the United States.

whereas.text: Whereas initiation of substance use during adolescence is associated with substance use and misuse in adulthood;
Whereas, in 2024, an estimated 58,000,000 people in the United States aged 12 and older participated in binge drinking;
Whereas, in 2024, more than 73,000,000 or 1 in 4 people in the United States aged 12 and older used an illicit drug, including cocaine and methamphetamine;
Whereas, in 2024, an estimated 48,000,000 people in the United States aged 12 and older had a substance use disorder;
Whereas, in 2024, an estimated 28,000,000 people in the United States aged 12 and older had an illicit drug use disorder;
Whereas, in 2024, an estimated 9,000,000 people in the United States aged 12 and older received substance use disorder treatment; and
Whereas, in 2024, more than 23,000,000 people in the United States aged 12 and older were in recovery from a substance use problem: Now, therefore, be it

paragraph.text: effective programs to prevent substance use and misuse;
programs to help stem the drug addiction and overdose epidemic in the United States; and
the designation of October 2025 as Substance Use & Misuse Prevention Month.
